docs.title: Home
docs.text:
A simple and efficient networking library.

Guides Reference

docs.title: Better API
docs.text:
local Net = require(Packages.net).Client
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")

MyEvent:Fire("Hello server")
MyEvent:Listen(function()
    print("Listening to the server!")
end)

Has some of the best API out all of the non-buffer networking libraries available, with no caveats.

docs.title: Easy Type Validation
docs.text:
local Net = require(Packages.net).Server
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")

MyEvent:Listen(function(number, string, boolean)
    print("Only accepting numbers, strings, and bools")
end, {"number", "string", "boolean"}) -- If the client does not send these types in that order, the packet will be dropped

Provides better type validation API than other libraries like Red, which require lots of extra code.

docs.title: Built-In Ratelimiting
docs.text:
local Net = require(Packages.net).Server
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")

MyEvent:Listen(function()
    print("Prints 1 time every 10 seconds")
end)

MyEvent:SetRateLimit(1, 10, function(sender)
    print(sender.DisplayName, "is sending too many requests")
end)

Has a built-in ratelimiting API which uses an efficient and reliable module under the hood.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • Fixed an issue where Futures were still used that resulted in errornous code

docs.title: Removed
docs.text: • Removed Futures, all code now yields

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Fixed issues with wally package types
  • Fixed changelog problems

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Added proper docs
  • Futures in replacement of Promises
Initial release! 🥳

docs.title: Roblox
docs.text:
1. Go to the latest releases page
  2. Download Standalone.rbxm
  3. Select Insert from file, and find Standalone.rbxm in your designated downloads folder

docs.title: Wally
docs.text:
1. Open a project in your favorite text editor
  2. Add lumin/net@^0.4.0 to your wally.toml file
  3. Run wally install the project should be inside of your /Packages directory.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Setup is relatively easy when using the Net package. It is recommended to be used in conjunction with Lumin Framework, but it can also be used without it.

First and foremost, you must require the package on both the server and the client in order to start the networking process without any errors or yielding. The most favorable format is to declare the .Server or .Client interface depending on the current context.

docs.title: Server
docs.text: local Net = require(Packages.net).Server

docs.title: Client
docs.text: local Net = require(Packages.net).Client

docs.title: Events
docs.text: Creating events is arguably the most important part of the entire library, and most useful obviously.

docs.title: Firing
docs.text:
There are many functions for firing that come with the package already. These include:

  • Fire
  • FireNow
  • FireAll
  • FireExpect
  • FireFilter

The difference between Fire and FireNow is a bit complicated but it will be extremely important to pick the correct when later during development.

For one, Fire will batch all of your calls into one single RemoteEvent:Fire call. A result of this is that all of your calls will be recieved by the server or client at the exact same time if called on the same frame. This can lead to issues like race conditions if you have a system that requires precision and relies on an event be called first. FireNow does not batch the calls, and will simply just send the call as soon as possible. This way, the order you sent the packets in will still persist on the other side. The downside of using this is that there is no optimization for this, so use it sparingly and only when needed!

A quick example of firing an event is as follows:

docs.title: Client
docs.text:
local Net = require(Packages.net).Client
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")

MyEvent:Fire("Hello server")
MyEvent:FireNow("Hello server")

docs.title: Server
docs.text:
local Net = require(Packages.net).Server
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")
local Player = game.Players:WaitForChild("Someone")

MyEvent:Fire(Player, "Hello client")
MyEvent:FireNow(Player, "Hello client")

-- Server exclusive
MyEvent:FireAll("Hello client")
MyEvent:FireExcept(Player, "Hello client")
MyEvent:FireFilter("Hello client", function(player)
    if player:IsA("Player") then
        return true
    else
        return false
    end
end)

docs.title: Listening
docs.text: Listening will persist the same across both the client and server. The only difference is that there is an additional sender argument on the server callback. Here's an example of how both would work.

docs.title: Client
docs.text:
local Net = require(Packages.net).Client
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")

MyEvent:Listen(function(arg)
    print(arg)
end)

docs.title: Server
docs.text:
local Net = require(Packages.net).Server
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")

MyEvent:Listen(function(sender, arg)
    print(sender.DisplayName, arg)
end)

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Functions are similar to how RemoteFunctions work and also act as a better way to perform tasks where the server returns data back to the client in a call.

docs.title: Invoking
docs.text:
Invoking is done on the client, and it returns a Future. Typically, you would wait for the Future to complete the job and to get the value. Here's an example using Await.

local Net = require(Packages.net).Client
local MyFunction = Net.Function("MyFunction")
local Response = MyFunction:InvokeAsync("Hi")

print(Response) -- Output: Goodbye

docs.title: Listening To Invocation
docs.text:
When listening to an invocation you must return a value. This is what happens on the server, and if you don't return a value it defeats the purpose of using a function. Use an event instead for this purpose!

local Net = require(Packages.net).Client
local MyFunction = Net.Function("MyFunction")

MyFunction:OnInvoke(function(sender, data)
    if data == "Hi" then
        return "Goodbye"
    end
    return "Huh?"
end)

docs.title: Ratelimiting
docs.text: The ratelimit feature is built in to every single event and allows you to specify a specific amount of packets that the server should recieve from the client per time interval.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
The usage of the ratelimiting function is very simple. An example is shown below with commenting.

local Net = require(Packages.net).Server
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")

MyEvent:Listen(function()
    print("Prints 1 time every 10 seconds")
end)

-- First number is the max calls per interval
-- Second number is the interval
MyEvent:SetRateLimit(1, 10, function(sender)
    print(sender.DisplayName, "is sending too many requests")
end)

docs.title: Type Validation
docs.text: The type validation feature allows you to strictly specify what types that the server should be getting from the client. For most cases, this ensures safety on both ends of code and also prevents exploiters from just guessing. This can be used to ban/kick them as well.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
To use the type validation feature, you have to declare a table of types after a server Listen or OnInvoke method. The types must also be in the exact order as they are sent; if they are not you might encounter some unexplainable issues if not tested. The example below will kick the sender of the packet if the type is invalid.

local Net = require(Packages.net).Server
local MyEvent = Net.Event("MyEvent")

MyEvent:Listen(function(sender, data1, data2, data3)
    print(sender.DisplayName, data1, data2, data3)
end, {"string", "Instance", "number"})

MyEvent:SetInvalidTypeCallback(function(sender)
    sender:Kick("You might be exploiting...")
end)

For a scenario when the player is kicked;

  • Data1 - string
  • Data2 - boolean
  • Data3 - number

A warning will be sent in the server output and the packet will be dropped. If you manually set an invalid type callback that will be run as well.

docs.title: LuminNet
docs.text: The main class of LuminNet.

docs.title: Client
docs.text:
A reference that points to the client sided version of the library

  • Client

docs.title: Server
docs.text:
A reference that points to the server sided version of the library

  • Server

docs.title: Interface
docs.text: References the client event and function constructors

docs.title: Event
docs.text:
Creates a new remote event

Parameters

  • name: string The name of the remote event to reference or create

  • reliable: boolean Whether the remote event should be reliable or not. Defaults to true

Returns

  • Event

docs.title: Function
docs.text:
Creates a new remote function

Parameters

  • name: string The name of the remote function to reference or create

  • reliable: boolean Whether the remote function should be reliable or not. Defaults to true This currently has no effect on remote functions

Returns

  • Function

docs.title: Event
docs.text: A client-sided event.

docs.title: Reliable
docs.text:
Whether or not the event uses a reliable remote event.

  • boolean

docs.title: Fire
docs.text:
Fires an event which sends data to the server, equivalent to RemoteEvent:FireServer.

Parameters

  • data: ...any The data that should be sent to the server

Returns

  • void

docs.title: FireNow
docs.text:
Same as Fire but does not batch the call / wait until the next frame.

Parameters

  • data: ...any The data that should be sent to the server

Returns

  • void

docs.title: Listen
docs.text:
Listens for the event to be fired by the server, then runs the provided function.

Parameters

  • listener: (...: any) -> () The function to call when data is recieved

Returns

  • void

docs.title: Function
docs.text: A client-sided function.

docs.title: Reliable
docs.text:
Whether or not the function uses a reliable remote event.

  • boolean

docs.title: InvokeAsync
docs.text:
Invokes the server, equivalent to RemoteFunction:InvokeServer. Returns a Future.

Parameters

  • data: ...any The data to invoke the server with

Returns

  • Future

docs.title: Interface
docs.text: References the server event and function constructors

docs.title: Event
docs.text:
Creates a new remote event

Parameters

  • name: string\ The name of the remote event to reference or create

  • reliable: boolean\ Whether the remote event should be reliable or not. Defaults to true

Returns

  • Event

docs.title: Function
docs.text:
Creates a new remote function

Parameters

  • name: string\ The name of the remote function to reference or create

  • reliable: boolean\ Whether the remote function should be reliable or not. Defaults to true\ This currently has no effect on remote functions

Returns

  • Function

docs.title: Event
docs.text: A server-sided event.

docs.title: Reliable
docs.text:
Whether or not the network event uses a reliable remote event.

  • boolean

docs.title: Fire
docs.text:
Fires an event which sends data to the client, equivalent to RemoteEvent:FireClient.

Parameters

  • recipient: Player The player who should recieve the data and/or call

  • data: ...any The data that should be sent to the client

Returns

  • void

docs.title: FireNow
docs.text:
Same as Fire but does not batch the call / wait until the next frame.

Parameters

  • recipient: Player The player who should recieve the data and/or call

  • data: ...any The data that should be sent to the client

Returns

  • void

docs.title: FireAll
docs.text:
Fires an event which sends data to every client connected to the server, equivalent RemoteEvent:FireAllClients.

Parameters

  • data: ...any The data that should be sent to each player

Returns

  • void

docs.title: FireExcept
docs.text:
Fires an event which sends data to every client connected to the server, except for players defined in the except parameter.

Parameters

  • except: { Player } | Player The players which the call should not be sent to

  • data: ...any The data that should be sent to each player except except

Returns

  • void

docs.title: FireFilter
docs.text:
Fires an event with a filter function, and runs the provided filter on every player in the server.

Parameters

  • filter: (Player) -> boolean The filter to run on each player, return a boolean to indicate that the player meets the threshold

  • data: ...any The data that should be sent to each player that meets the threshold for filter

Returns

  • void

docs.title: Listen
docs.text:
Listens for the event to be fired by the client, then runs the provided function.

Parameters

  • listener: (sender: Player, ...: any) -> () The function to call when data is recieved

  • validTypes: { string }? Valid types that the server should recieve in the order they are specified

Returns

  • void

docs.title: SetRateLimit
docs.text:
Sets a rate limit that is applied when firing an event from the client.

Parameters

  • maxCalls: number The maximum amount of invokes allowed every interval seconds; set to -1 to disable the rate limit

  • resetInterval: number? The interval of which maxCalls is reset

  • overflowCallback: (sender: Player) -> ()? The callback function to run when the player has exceeded the current rate limit

Returns

  • void

docs.title: SetInvalidTypeCallback
docs.text:
Allows you to set a callback that runs whenever an invalid type is sent by the client.

Parameters

  • callback: (sender: Player) -> () The function to run when an invalid type is reached and a packet is dropped

Returns

  • void

docs.title: Function
docs.text: A server-sided function.

docs.title: Reliable
docs.text:
Whether or not the function uses a reliable remote event.

  • boolean

docs.title: OnInvoke
docs.text:
Recieves an invoke from the client, and runs the callback function which returns some data. Equivalent to RemoteFunction.OnServerInvoke.

Parameters

  • callback: (sender: Player, ...: any) -> (any, ...any) The callback function to run on invoke, must return at least 1 value

  • validTypes: { string }? Valid types that the server should recieve in the order they are specified

Returns

  • void

docs.title: SetRateLimit
docs.text:
Sets a rate limit that is applied when firing an event from the client.

Parameters

  • maxCalls: number The maximum amount of invokes allowed every interval seconds; set to -1 to disable the rate limit

  • resetInterval: number? The interval of which maxCalls is reset

  • overflowCallback: (sender: Player) -> ()? The callback function to run when the player has exceeded the current rate limit

Returns

  • void

docs.title: SetInvalidTypeCallback
docs.text:
Allows you to set a callback that runs whenever an invalid type is sent by the client.

Parameters

  • callback: (sender: Player) -> () The function to run when an invalid type is reached and a packet is dropped

Returns

  • void
